# devhelm_status_page_component_group (Resource)

Manages a single component group on a DevHelm status page.

Renames are safe: changing the HCL address preserves the underlying group UUID, so subscribers, incident history, and `devhelm_status_page_component.group_id` references stay intact. Use Terraform's built-in `moved {}` block to rename the resource address.

## Example

```hcl
resource "devhelm_status_page_component_group" "infra" {
  status_page_id = devhelm_status_page.public.id
  name           = "Infrastructure"
  description    = "Core platform services."
}
```

### Bulk creation with `for_each`

```hcl
locals {
  groups = {
    infra = { name = "Infrastructure" }
    apps  = { name = "Applications" }
  }
}

resource "devhelm_status_page_component_group" "g" {
  for_each       = local.groups
  status_page_id = devhelm_status_page.public.id
  name           = each.value.name
}
```

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "devhelm_status_page_component_group" "infra" {
  status_page_id = devhelm_status_page.public.id
  name           = "Infrastructure"
  description    = "Core platform services."
}

resource "devhelm_status_page_component_group" "apps" {
  status_page_id = devhelm_status_page.public.id
  name           = "Applications"
  display_order  = 1
  collapsed      = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Group display name shown above its components
- `status_page_id` (String) ID of the parent status page. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `collapsed` (Boolean) Whether the group is collapsed by default (default: true)
- `description` (String) Optional description shown below the group title. Omit the attribute to clear an existing description; empty string is rejected (use omission instead, since the API normalizes "" → null on write).
- `display_order` (Number) Position in the group list (lower = earlier). Server-assigned if omitted.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Group UUID assigned by the API

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Component groups are imported as <status_page_id>/<group_id>.
terraform import devhelm_status_page_component_group.infra \
  7f819203-4567-89ab-cdef-012345678901/8091a2b3-4567-89cd-ef01-234567890123
```
